---
title: "T Cell Medium (RPMI + 10% FCS + Supplements)"
subtitle: "Abdullah Lab, IMMEI, University Hospital Bonn"
protocol_id: "BUF-TCELL-MED"
version: "v1.1"
date: "2026-04-15"
description: "Complete RPMI 1640 medium supplemented with FCS, Penicillin/Streptomycin, beta-mercaptoethanol, and L-glutamine for mouse T cell and NLC culture. Optional supplements: sodium pyruvate, HEPES, MEM NEAAs."
---

**Protocol ID:** {< meta protocol_id >}  
**Version:** {< meta version >}  
**Author:** Dillon Corvino  

# Purpose

Complete T cell medium based on RPMI 1640, supplemented for optimal survival, activation, and expansion of mouse T cells and NLCs in vitro.

\tableofcontents

# Linked protocols

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

library(tidyverse)

this_buffer <- rmarkdown::metadata$protocol_id
deps <- read_csv(here::here("protocols_src/_dependencies.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

linked <- deps |>
  filter(buffer_id == this_buffer) |>
  distinct(protocol_id)

if (nrow(linked) == 0) {
  cat("None\n")
} else {
  linked |>
    mutate(line = glue::glue("- `{protocol_id}`")) |>
    pull(line) |>
    cat(sep = "\n")
}
```

---

# Working buffer composition

| Component                 | Final concentration | Notes                          |
|---------------------------|--------------------|--------------------------------|
| RPMI 1640                 | —                  | Base medium                   |
| Fetal calf serum (FCS)    | 10% (v/v)          | Heat-inactivated              |
| Penicillin/Streptomycin   | 1% (v/v)           | 100x stock → 1x final         |
| L-glutamine               | 2 mM               | From 200 mM stock; prefer GlutaMAX if medium will be stored at 4°C for weeks — L-glutamine degrades over time |
| Sodium pyruvate           | 1 mM               | From 100 mM stock; **optional** |
| beta-mercaptoethanol      | 50 uM              | From 50 mM stock              |
| HEPES                     | 10 mM              | From 1 M stock; **optional** but common |
| MEM Non-essential amino acids (NEAAs) | 1% (v/v) | 100x stock → 1x final; **optional** |

::: {.callout-note}
**Sodium pyruvate, HEPES, and NEAAs** are optional but improve viability in longer cultures and under stress conditions. Worth including for multiday assays (>48 h) or when cells will be under metabolic stress (e.g. cytokine stimulation, limiting nutrients).
:::

::: {.callout-note}
**Beta-mercaptoethanol** is particularly important for mouse primary cells, which are more sensitive to oxidative stress than human cell lines. Do not omit for primary mouse T cells or NLCs.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
**GlutaMAX vs L-glutamine:** If medium will be stored at 4°C for more than 1–2 weeks, substitute GlutaMAX at the same volume — L-glutamine degrades to pyroglutamate and ammonia over time, which is toxic at high concentrations. GlutaMAX is stable and converted to glutamine by cells.
:::

# Preparation

## Stock solutions required

| Reagent                    | Stock concentration | Dilution to final | Optional |
|----------------------------|--------------------|------------------|---------|
| Fetal calf serum (FCS)     | —                  | 10% (v/v)        | No |
| Penicillin/Streptomycin    | 100x               | 1:100            | No |
| L-glutamine (or GlutaMAX)  | 200 mM             | 1:100            | No |
| Sodium pyruvate            | 100 mM             | 1:100            | Yes |
| beta-mercaptoethanol       | 50 mM              | 1:1000           | No |
| HEPES                      | 1 M                | 1:100            | Yes |
| MEM NEAAs                  | 100x               | 1:100            | Yes |
| RPMI 1640                  | —                  | Base medium      | No |

## Volume calculations

Dilution logic:

- 10% FCS → 1:10
- 1% Pen/Strep (100x) → 1:100
- 2 mM L-glut from 200 mM → 1:100
- 1 mM sodium pyruvate from 100 mM → 1:100 *(optional)*
- 50 uM beta-mercaptoethanol from 50 mM → 1:1000
- 10 mM HEPES from 1 M → 1:100 *(optional)*
- 1x NEAAs from 100x → 1:100 *(optional)*

## Preparation table

Volumes for mandatory components. Add optional supplements (pyruvate, HEPES, NEAAs) by substituting equivalent volume from the RPMI column.

| Reagent              | 10 mL | 50 mL | 100 mL | 200 mL | 500 mL |
|----------------------|-------|-------|--------|--------|--------|
| RPMI (mL)            | 8.69  | 43.45 | 86.9   | 173.8  | 434.5  |
| FCS (mL)             | 1.0   | 5.0   | 10.0   | 20.0   | 50.0   |
| Pen/Strep (uL)       | 100   | 500   | 1,000  | 2,000  | 5,000  |
| L-glut / GlutaMAX (uL) | 100 | 500  | 1,000  | 2,000  | 5,000  |
| beta-ME (uL)         | 10    | 50    | 100    | 200    | 500    |
| Pyruvate (uL) *opt*  | 100   | 500   | 1,000  | 2,000  | 5,000  |
| HEPES (uL) *opt*     | 100   | 500   | 1,000  | 2,000  | 5,000  |
| NEAAs (uL) *opt*     | 100   | 500   | 1,000  | 2,000  | 5,000  |

Label with:
   - *T Cell Complete Medium*  
   - Date of preparation  
   - Expiry (4 weeks after preparation)

::: callout-tip
Add beta-mercaptoethanol fresh where possible for optimal T cell viability.
:::

# Storage and stability

- Store at **4 °C**, protected from light.  
- Use within **4 weeks** after preparation.  
- Do not freeze.

# Reagent details

| Reagent                 | Supplier | Cat. # | Notes |
|-------------------------|----------|--------|-------|
| RPMI 1640               | Various  | —      | Base medium |
| Fetal calf serum        | Various  | —      | Heat-inactivated |
| Pen/Strep (100x)        | Various  | —      | Antibiotics |
| L-glutamine (200 mM)    | Various  | —      | Degrades over weeks at 4°C; substitute GlutaMAX for long-stored medium |
| GlutaMAX (200 mM)       | Various  | —      | Stable dipeptide alternative to L-glutamine; use at same volume |
| Sodium pyruvate (100 mM)| Various  | —      | Optional |
| beta-mercaptoethanol    | Various  | —      | Handle in hood; essential for mouse primary cells |
| HEPES (1 M)             | Various  | —      | Optional; buffering capacity for longer assays |
| MEM NEAAs (100x)        | Various  | —      | Optional; supports viability in multiday cultures |

# Safety

- Handle medium aseptically in a biosafety cabinet.  
- beta-mercaptoethanol is toxic and volatile; handle in a chemical hood.  
- Dispose of expired or contaminated medium as biohazardous waste.

# Version history

| Version | Date       | Author         | Changes |
|--------|------------|----------------|---------|
| v1.0   | 2026-03-02 | Dillon Corvino | Initial T cell medium document |
| v1.1   | 2026-04-15 | Dillon Corvino | Added HEPES (optional, 10 mM) and MEM NEAAs (optional, 1x) to composition; marked sodium pyruvate as optional; added notes on GlutaMAX vs L-glutamine stability, beta-ME importance for mouse primary cells, and conditions where optional supplements are recommended |
